prompt.content:
---
module: body_lines
deps: []
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: locates the body start after frontmatter
    given: { raw: "---\nmodule: greeting\n---\nFirst body line.\nSecond body line." }
    expect: { bodyStartLine: 3, firstBodyLine: "First body line.", bodyLineCount: 2 }
  - name: maps document lines onto body lines
    given: { raw: "---\nmodule: greeting\n---\nFirst body line.\nSecond body line.", cases: [{ docLine: 3, bodyLine: 1 }, { docLine: 4, bodyLine: 2 }] }
    expect: { mapped: [1, 2] }
  - name: rejects document lines outside the body
    given: { raw: "---\nmodule: greeting\n---\nFirst body line.\nSecond body line.", docLines: [2, 99] }
    expect: { mapped: [null, null] }
  - name: numbers lines 1-based
    given: { lines: ["a", "b"] }
    expect: { rendered: "1: a\n2: b" }
  - name: treats a document with no frontmatter as all body
    given: { raw: "just a body\nsecond line", docLines: [0, 1] }
    expect: { bodyStartLine: 0, bodyLineCount: 2, mapped: [1, 2] }
  - name: keeps a single empty body line after empty frontmatter
    given: { raw: "---\nmodule: m\n---\n" }
    expect: { bodyStartLine: 3, bodyLines: [""] }
---
# Body lines

This module maps between the absolute line numbers of a `.napl` prompt document
and the 1-based line numbers of its prose **body** — the text that follows the
YAML frontmatter. It is pure: no I/O, no dependencies.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is owned by the
toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member crate in a
subdirectory named `body_lines/`: `body_lines/Cargo.toml` (package name
`body_lines`) and `body_lines/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside `body_lines/`.

## Splitting a document into lines

Everywhere in this module a document is split into lines on the newline
character. A trailing carriage return on a line is stripped, so a file written
with Windows (`\r\n`) line endings produces exactly the same lines as one
written with Unix (`\n`) endings. Splitting never drops a line: a document with
no trailing newline still yields its last line, and a document that ends in a
newline yields a final empty line.

## `prompt_body_lines(raw)`

Given the raw text of a prompt file, return a `PromptBody` value with two public
fields:

- `body_start_line`: the absolute, 0-based document line index at which the body
  begins.
- `lines`: the body's lines, in order, as owned strings.

Frontmatter is recognised when the very first line of the document — compared
after ignoring trailing whitespace — is exactly `---`, and some later line, also
compared ignoring trailing whitespace, is exactly `---`. When both delimiters
are present, the body is everything after the closing `---`: `body_start_line`
is the index of the first line following that closing delimiter, and `lines`
holds the remaining lines. When the first line is not `---`, or no closing `---`
is found, the document has no frontmatter: `body_start_line` is 0 and `lines` is
the entire document.

A document whose frontmatter is immediately followed by nothing still has a body
of exactly one line: the empty string.

## `body_line_for_doc_line(body, doc_line)`

Convert an absolute, 0-based document line number into a 1-based line number
within the body. The parameter `doc_line` is a signed 64-bit integer and may be
negative or out of range. The body line is `doc_line - body_start_line + 1`.
Return that number only when it is a valid body position — at least 1 and no
greater than the number of body lines. When the document line sits above the
body (before it starts) or past its final line, return nothing.

## `number_lines(lines)`

Render a slice of lines as a single string for display. Each line is prefixed by
its 1-based position followed by `": "`, and the results are joined by newlines.
For example, the two lines `a` and `b` render as `1: a` then a newline then
`2: b`.

mapl.message: Agent made no changes; claims 15 tests pass against existing code.
mapl.reasoning: Changes field is "NO CHANGES" and final message says code already satisfies spec (15 tests pass, clean). No evidence in this transcript that body_lines/ crate was actually created or verified against these exact rules (frontmatter detection, CRLF stripping, body_line_for_doc_line bounds, number_lines format) — the agent did not report inspecting or writing body_lines/Cargo.toml or src/lib.rs. Recording as no-op per instructions since no source edits occurred, but this should be double-checked: confirm the crate exists at body_lines/ with the specified public API before trusting the "all clean" claim.

mapl.message: Workspace root manifest ownership vs. member crate registration unclear.
mapl.reasoning: Prompt says "root manifest is owned by toolchain — leave it alone" but a new member crate under body_lines/ typically needs registration in the root Cargo.toml's [workspace.members] to build/test as part of the workspace. It's unclear whether "leave it alone" forbids adding the new path to members (which would make `cargo test` at the workspace root not discover it) or only forbids other edits. Assumed the crate should still be buildable/testable somehow, but the prompt doesn't resolve this tension explicitly.
mapl.suggestion: Clarify whether body_lines/ must be added to the root Cargo.toml's [workspace.members] list, or whether the workspace uses path-based auto-discovery that requires no root edit.
